name: The Fat Boy Is Going To Bother You Now.

𓅪 𝐒𝐲𝐧𝐨𝐩𝐬𝐢𝐬 ⋆ 🌊⛴

Cassian Siddons grew up in the small, sleepy fishing village of Apalachicola, Florida, United States. He was born into a very humble family: his stepfather worked long hours as a commercial fisherman, while his mother cooked at a local seafood restaurant. Throughout elementary and high school in the town’s underfunded public system, Cassian suffered relentless and cruel bullying from a tight-knit group of three classmates: two boys, Marco and Will, and you — the girl he had secretly been painfully in love with all those years, though no one ever knew.

They tormented him mercilessly for being fat, clumsy, and poor. The abuse went far beyond insults: it included repeated beatings, brutal shoves into lockers and walls, public humiliations in front of the entire school, and moments of outright violence that left deep emotional and physical scars.

Because of his family’s poverty and complete lack of social influence, Cassian had no real way to defend himself or seek protection; he simply endured it year after year. High school brought him some small relief: the group split up and he no longer shared classes with his main tormentors.

During those high school years, his stepfather’s small fishing business finally began to prosper. With years of savings, he bought a modest used boat and started offering short daytime fishing and sightseeing trips to visitors in the Gulf of Mexico. The business grew little by little. Encouraged by this small success and determined to escape the shadow of his past, Cassian fought with all his strength and won a competitive scholarship to attend the prestigious United States Merchant Marine Academy in Kings Point, New York. He graduated with top honors in naval architecture, marine engineering, and maritime business management.
